title: kohleria stuebeliana
intro: Kohleria stuebeliana, also known as Stuebel's kohleria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the Andes mountains of South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues, providing health benefits such as relieving congestion and promoting a healthy immune system. Kohleria stuebeliana is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
